title: DSDS Theme Definitions
description: A theme documents an alternative set of token values: a color mode, a density setting, or a brand variant. DSDS captures the purpose, document blocks, and relationships of a theme. The override values live in the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) file that the `source` property points to. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Put internal artifact links in the `links` metadata field, not in a dedicated document block kind.

tokenOverride.description: A single token override inside a theme: which token changes, and why. The override value lives in the DTCG file. This entry records the intent and reason.

token.description: The identifier of the token being overridden. MUST match the `identifier` of a token defined elsewhere in the system. Token identifiers follow no fixed pattern, so they can support DTCG and design tool naming styles (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary').

description.description: Says what changes for this token in this theme and why (e.g., 'Lightened to meet WCAG AAA contrast on the dark background'). The resolved value lives in the DTCG file.

theme.description: A named context that adapts the design system's token values. DSDS captures the purpose, usage document blocks, accessibility notes, and the list of affected tokens. The override values live in the DTCG file that `source` points to. Use themes for color modes (light, dark, high-contrast), density settings (compact, comfortable), brand variants, or product-specific tweaks. The token catalog stays the source of truth for token identity, type, and docs. The `metadata` object holds optional fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a theme.

name.description: Display name for the theme (e.g., 'Dark Mode', 'High Contrast', 'Compact Density').

description.description: What this theme is for, the context it adapts the system to, and when to apply it. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

source.description: Points to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not copy token values. The DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type info.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this theme's token override definitions.

path.description: The dot-path to this theme's group within the DTCG file, if applicable.

overrides.description: Token overrides this theme applies. Each entry says which token changes and why. List only tokens whose values differ from the default. Unlisted tokens keep their default values. The override values live in the DTCG file that `source` points to.

relationships.description: Typed dependency edges from this theme to other entities — e.g. 'depends-on' the tokens it overrides, 'extends' a base theme. Inverse edges are derived by tooling.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this theme, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Themes accept the general kinds only (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters, so tools SHOULD keep it for display.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for agents (AI/LLM) only — a separate space that accepts the same block kinds as `documentBlocks`. `documentBlocks` is the default: put anything a human reader needs there. Use `agentDocumentBlocks` only for content that helps an agent build correctly but is noise for humans. For example: hard MUST/MUST-NOT rules an agent should treat as firm limits; notes that correct a mistake an agent is likely to make, such as reaching for this entity when a confusable one fits better; constraints backed by test-run evidence; and machine-checkable criteria with verification modes. A hard rule that humans also need (such as an accessibility requirement) belongs in `documentBlocks`, not here. Agent blocks add to the human docs — they never replace or duplicate them. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing docs. Agents SHOULD read both arrays, human blocks first.
